author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
kabe āhā gaurāṅga baliyā
bhojana⋅śayane, dehera jatana,
chāḍiba virakta hañā

navadvīpa⋅dhāme, nagare nagare,
abhimāna parihari’
dhāmavāsī⋅ghare, mādhukarī la’ba,
khāiba udara bhari’

nadī-taṭe giyā, añjali añjali,
piba prabhu⋅padajala
taru-tale paḍi’, ālasya tyajiba,
pāiba śarīre bala

kākuti kariyā, ‘gaura⋅gadādhara’,
‘śrī⋅rādhā⋅mādhava’⋅nāma
kā̐diyā kā̐diyā, ḍāki’ uccarave,
bhramiba sakala dhāma

vaiṣṇava dekhiyā, paḍiba caraṇe,
hṛdayera bandhu jāni’
vaiṣṇava ṭhākura, ‘prabhura kīrtana’,
dekhāibe dāsa māni’
en_translation:
Alas, when, chanting 'Gaurāṅga', will I become detached and give up caring for my body in eating and sleeping?

In Navadvīpa-dhāma, from town to town, abandoning false pride, I will beg alms (madhukarī) at the homes of the residents of the dhāma and eat to fill my belly.

Going to the riverbank, palmful by palmful, I will drink the water of the Lord's feet. Lying under a tree, I will give up laziness and gain strength in my body.

Piteously chanting the names 'Gaura-Gadādhara', 'Śrī Rādhā-Mādhava', weeping and weeping, calling out loudly, I will wander throughout the dhāma.

Seeing a Vaiṣṇava, I will fall at his feet, knowing him to be a friend of my heart. The Vaiṣṇava Ṭhākura, accepting me as his servant, will show me the Lord's kīrtana.
